g.data-narration: Welcome to our comprehensive guide on Network Security Models. Let's explore how to protect digital assets.
Network security protects data from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.
g.text: Network security is critical in modern computing, protecting data and
communication from unauthorized access and cyber threats.
Key: CIA Triad - Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability

g.data-narration: By the end of this lesson, you will understand security threats, OSI security model, and network protection components.
g.text: 1. Define security threats: Interception, Interruption, Modification, Fabrication
2. Explain the OSI Security Model across all 7 layers
3. Describe network security components: Segmentation, Firewalls, IDPS, VPNs

g.data-narration: There are four main types of security threats that compromise network security systems worldwide.
g.text: Four main types compromise network security:

g.data-narration: Interception occurs when unauthorized parties gain access to confidential information through eavesdropping.
Interruption disrupts normal network or system functioning, like denial-of-service attacks overwhelming servers.
Modification happens when unauthorized parties alter data or system configurations, such as malware injection.
Fabrication creates fake data or messages, like spoofing attacks that appear from legitimate sources.
The OSI security model helps identify security threats at each layer of the 7-layer OSI protocol stack.
g.text: 7 layers to identify and address security threats at each level of the protocol stack:

g.data-narration: Physical layer needs physical security; Data Link layer faces MAC spoofing; Network layer deals with IP spoofing attacks.
Transport layer faces SYN flooding; Session has hijacking risks; Presentation needs encryption; Application deals with malware.
A comprehensive network security model includes network segmentation, firewalls, IDPS systems, and VPNs.
Network segmentation divides networks into isolated segments using VLANs to reduce attack surfaces.
g.text: isolated segments to reduce attack surface

g.data-narration: IDPS monitors network traffic for threats; VPNs encrypt traffic between endpoints for secure communication.
Key points: Understand the four threat types, know OSI layer security concerns, and implement comprehensive protection.
g.text: • OSI Model: Security at each layer addresses specific threats

g.data-narration: In conclusion, implementing these security measures protects networks and ensures data confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
g.text: A comprehensive network security model includes:
1. Understanding security threats (Interception, Interruption, Modification, Fabrication)
2. OSI Security Model addressing threats at each layer
3. Network security components: Segmentation, Firewalls, IDPS, VPNs
Protect your networks: Implement multiple security layers!

g.data-narration: The CIA Triad forms the foundation of information security: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability.
g.text: The CIA Triad - Foundation of Security

g.data-narration: For exam success: memorize threat types, OSI layers with concerns, and network security components.
